title: 三只瞎老鼠
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\151-三只瞎老鼠-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
After Mrs. Bailey leaves to knit a sweater, Mr. Palladini behaves lecherously toward Molly, causing her discomfort. Mr. Paravicini advises Molly and her husband that they cannot trust people easily and questions whether they checked the references of their tenants. While observing Mr. Palladini stoking the fire, Molly realizes he uses his back to hide the fact that he uses makeup to appear younger.

Major Metcalf enters to report that a water pipe in the downstairs cloakroom has frozen. When Molly mentions that the police are sending an officer, Major Metcalf reacts with a strange, mask-like expression of fear or excitement. Jules arrives and notes that the heavy snow should make the roads impassable, but a man on skis suddenly knocks on the bay window. The newcomer is Sheriff Trotter, a young officer. Following his arrival, Mr. Paravicini angrily asks Molly why she called the police, though she denies doing so. Christopher Raine notes that the group is now completely isolated from the outside world.

Major Metcalf attempts to use the telephone but finds it broken. Jules leads Sheriff Trotter and Molly to the study to conduct separate questioning. Trotter explains that his arrival is a security measure related to the death of a woman named Madame Lyon, which occurred two days prior in London. He clarifies that the victim's real name was Molline Gregg, the wife of a farmer named John Gregg from Longridge Farm.
